text:
ani 19
Bhadra
Aslesha, Jyeshtha, Revati Budha 17
The same group recurs after every twenty seven minutes.
Each group of three constellations is divided into movable
(cardinal), fixed, and common signs. This is not exactly so.
Krittika first quarter, end of Aslesha, third quarter of
Visakha, and the second quarter ofDhanishtta end the cardi
nal signs Mesha, Karka, Tula, and Makara respectively. Sec
ond quarter of Mrigasira, first quarter of Uttara Bhadra, end
of Jyeshtha, and the third quarter of Purvabhadra end the
fixed signs ofVrishabha, Simha, Vrischika, and Kumbha re
spectively. These distinctions have to be kept in mind in any
tabulation. Enumerating the nakshatras from Asvini to Re
vati by the numbers one to 27, we find the numbers
3,5,7,12,14,16,21,23 and 25 fall each in two different signs.
These constellations do not coincide with the division into
movable, fixed and common signs. These nakshatras partake
the natures of two signs. This again makes tabulation into the
